content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer ( N ) such that (N≤100)( N \leq 100 )(N≤100).
Your task is to output the **square** of the given number.


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains a single integer ( N ).


Output Format

Print the square of ( N ).
constraints: The result fits in a 32‑bit signed integer
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 100), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 100).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a line with the square of N.

solution.approach: Read the integer T. Loop T times, each time read N and compute N * N using 32‑bit integer arithmetic. Print the result on its own line. The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time multiplication per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
T = int(data[0])
out = []
idx = 1
for _ in range(T):
    N = int(data[idx])
    idx += 1
    out.append(str(N * N))
print('\n'.join(out))
